reason: QUEST dupilumab row: tN=631 vs cN=313 — actual QUEST randomized 1902 (1264 dupi + 638 placebo) so this is ~half the trial; tE/cE 175/293 also internally implausible (cE 293 of cN 313 = 93.6% event rate, while tE/tN=175/631=27.7%; ratio inverted vs RR=0.52).
ground_truth_hint: Castro et al NEJM 2018 QUEST PMID 29782224; cE and cN appear swapped

reason: ZONDA OR=4.12 labeled OR; computed OR from tE=50/tN=73, cE=18/cN=75 is ~6.87 — disagrees with published ZONDA OR for OCS reduction. cE/tE counts likely off.
ground_truth_hint: Nair et al NEJM 2017 ZONDA PMID 28530840; verify ZONDA OCS-reduction OR and arm counts

reason: XPORT-MF selinexor row has PMID 39911057 which resolves to Future Oncol 2025 'Selinexor plus ruxolitinib in JAK inhibitor treatment-naive myelofibrosis: SENTRY Phase 3 study design'. This is the SENTRY trial design paper, NOT the XPORT-MF readout. Trial-name/PMID mismatch within same molecule (selinexor-ruxolitinib) but wrong study.
ground_truth_hint: XPORT-MF-034 primary publication Tantravahi et al not yet matched; SENTRY is a different (phase 3) trial

reason: PERSIST-1 row: cE=0, cN=107, tE=41, tN=220 produces OR=39.51 with UCI=643.06. While numerically consistent with a zero-cell, an OR of 39.5 with CI ranging to 643 indicates the data were not corrected (no continuity correction applied) and the precision is uninterpretable — flag as effect-size-implausibility/extreme-sparse.
ground_truth_hint: Mascarenhas et al JAMA Oncol 2018 PERSIST-1 reports more granular spleen-volume response with continuity correction

reason: DISRUPT-PAD-3 row: tE=153 with tN=153 implies 100% event rate in the treatment arm (impossible for any clinically meaningful outcome), cE=90/cN=153. RR=1.82 and CI tight (1.55-2.13) suggest data are summary of procedural success not a clinical event — schema labeled RR with implausible 100% rate.
ground_truth_hint: DISRUPT-PAD-3 reports angiographic success; reframe outcome or correct tE which should be < tN

evidence: tE=240/tN=240 = 100% event rate in experimental arm; cE=133/cN=240 = 55.4% in control. EAGLE published OS at primary analysis had ~67% deaths overall. The 100% rate in only one arm violates the equal-randomization expectation and arithmetic plausibility.
action: Re-extract tE from EAGLE primary; current value appears to be a transcription artifact (perhaps tN value duplicated into tE).

evidence: PMID 33121997 is 'Oxaliplatin-based adjuvant chemotherapy duration (3 vs 6 months) for stage II colon cancer: ACHIEVE-2 trial' (Ann Oncol 2020) — colon cancer, NOT the Orforglipron T2DM ACHIEVE-2 (Eli Lilly) extraction intended. Acronym-collision PMID assignment.
action: Replace with correct Orforglipron ACHIEVE-2 primary (Lilly NEJM 2025/2026).

evidence: TRUE-NORTH is a Ulcerative Colitis induction+maintenance trial of ozanimod, NOT a multiple sclerosis trial — but it is included in MS_S1P_BROAD_REVIEW. Same for ELEVATE-UC-12 and ELEVATE-UC-52 (etrasimod UC). The 'broad' framing of the review may justify mixing all S1P modulator indications, but the disease-mixing destroys interpretability of a pooled estimate and violates standard PICO discipline.
action: Either retitle the review to 'S1P modulators across indications' and stratify by disease, or remove UC trials from a MS-focused review.

evidence: BERLIN-VT publishedHR=1.65 (1.01-2.71) is reported with estimandType=HR — but BERLIN-VT primary (Willems 2020) showed VT ablation arm with HIGHER event rate due to early-trial-termination for futility on the composite endpoint. Sign of effect plausible. However group field says 'Catheter ablation vs AAD BERLIN-VT' and tE=50/79 vs cE=38/80 yields RR ≈ 1.33, NOT 1.65 — published HR was different endpoint (composite at last follow-up). Internal arithmetic on extracted events is inconsistent with stated HR — likely outcome definition mismatch between event-count fields and HR field.
action: Confirm whether (tE,cE) reflect the same outcome as the publishedHR. If different timepoints/composites, document explicitly.

evidence: PARTITA published N=517 total (Della Bella 2022 Circulation), randomized 1:1. The chunk shows tN=257 + cN=260 = 517 total — consistent. However tE=145/cE=195 with HR 0.71 (0.52-0.97) is the composite cardiac mortality/hospitalisation analysis — match is plausible. KEEP but note: PARTITA was published 2022, chunk says year=2024 — year field appears stale or refers to extended follow-up.
action: Verify year field; PARTITA primary was 2022 Circulation, not 2024.

evidence: Group field describes BIOLUX P-III as 'registry' but the NCT is registered as RCT. Mixing registry-derived event rates into an RCT-only NMA inflates the apparent effect direction with selection bias.
action: Either confirm BIOLUX P-III is RCT data or exclude registry data.

evidence: Extraction shows tE=150/tN=200/cE=50/cN=100 — all exact round numbers, RR=1.5 exact. The published ILLUMENATE Pivotal had irregular numbers (200 DCB / 100 PTA primary patency at 12mo). Round-number artefact suggests placeholder data.
cE=0/cN=0 indicates single-arm; publishedHR=null. Schema-clean but unflagged as single-arm; downstream pooling may incorrectly try to compute relative effect.
action: Add explicit single_arm=true flag or exclude from comparative pooling.

evidence: Same pattern: cE=0/cN=0, publishedHR=null. Single-arm Asian sacituzumab study. Needs flag.
Same pattern: cE=0/cN=0, publishedHR=null. PMID 41871716 confirmed correct via PubMed (Phase Ib TROPION-Lung02 paper). Mark as single-arm/non-comparative phase Ib readout.
action: Add single_arm flag; do not include in comparative pooling.

evidence: tE=251/tN=272 = 92.3% and cE=263/cN=271 = 97.0%. For PFS/OS at median follow-up these rates are at-end-of-study. Schema OK but require source confirmation.
action: Confirm follow-up time and that event rates are mature.

evidence: Trial 'name' GASTRIC-ESD-1 is a generic placeholder; the same pattern (GASTRIC-ESD-2, COLON-ESD-EMR, ESD-EMR-LSL, BARRETT-ESD, JCOG-0904-ESD) suggests synthesized/aliased names rather than original trial acronyms. NCT00566306 needs CT.gov verification — generic acronyms across an 8-trial set suggest auto-generated extraction labels.
action: Cross-check each NCT against ClinicalTrials.gov; if NCTs resolve to real but differently-named trials, restore canonical acronyms. If NCTs are confabulated, flag review for re-extraction.

evidence: Unable to spot-check PMID 29499220 in this audit pass (rate-limit / batch dedup). Flag for human follow-up — given chunk-wide PMID misattribution rate >40% the prior is high that this one is also wrong.

tE=14, tN=14 — 100% response rate is implausible at any psilocybin endpoint. Phase 2 psilocybin MDD trials show response ~60-80%. Also cN=13 with cE=8 (62%). Pair yields RR=1.97 which roughly matches but tE field is likely transcription error.

detail: Durvalumab GeparNuevo TNBC: extraction's publishedHR=1.55 [1.10,2.18] with tE/tN=49/88 vs cE/cN=31/86 again implies a 'non-pCR' framing (RR=1.54); published primary endpoint favoured durvalumab. Same systematic outcome-framing inversion as F-7-001/002.

KEYNOTE-756 pembrolizumab+chemo HR+/HER2- neoadjuvant: pCR rate published higher with pembro (~24.3% vs 15.6%). Extraction's RR=1.61 [1.27,2.04] from tE/cE=159/100 in identical-sized arms again uses non-pCR direction. Sign inverted relative to drug benefit.

PMID 38913933 cited for CheckMate-7FL is a 2024 review article (Expert Opin Biol Ther; Schlam et al., DOI 10.1080/14712598.2024.2370395) that *discusses* CheckMate-7FL and KEYNOTE-756, not the trial primary publication. According to PubMed. Secondary citation acceptable but suboptimal for a verification anchor. RR direction also same non-pCR framing as F-7-001.

Extraction labels NCT03726879 as 'IMpassion032' adjuvant TNBC atezolizumab. NCT03726879 is in fact the IMpassion031 / NSABP B-59-related adjuvant programme variant. IMpassion032 nomenclature is non-canonical; the published Roche atezolizumab adjuvant TNBC programme typically reads NSABP B-59 / GBG 96-GeparDouze (different NCT). NCT-to-name binding warrants verification — possible name-NCT mismatch.

PMID 24716680 verified (TOPCAT primary, NEJM 2014, DOI 10.1056/NEJMoa1313731). Per PubMed, full ITT N=3445 (1722 spironolactone / 1723 placebo). Published primary composite events 320 vs 351, HR 0.89 [0.77,1.04]. Extraction's tE/tN=320/1722, cE/cN=351/1723, HR 0.89 matches the FULL ITT, NOT the 'TOPCAT-Americas' subgroup labelled in the 'group' field. Group label 'TOPCAT-Americas' is inconsistent with the numbers (Americas subgroup N ~1767). Also, year=2017 in the extraction is wrong (true year 2014).

STEP-HFpEF semaglutide in obesity HFpEF reported a continuous primary (KCCQ score Δ ~7.8 points, weight Δ ~-10.7%). Extraction encodes a binary RR=1.81 [1.49,2.20] with tE/tN=195/263 vs cE/cN=109/266 — implies a binary responder analysis (e.g., ≥5-point KCCQ improvement). The 'publishedHR' label combined with estimandType='RR' is fine, but the magnitude (RR 1.81) is in the upper plausibility range for a responder definition without a stated threshold; flag for definition verification.

Trial labelled 'FREEDOM-HF' (Levosimendan / pump optimization HFpEF) under NCT05047536, year 2023. The canonical FREEDOM-HF acronym in cardiology refers to the older Levosimendan/IV outpatient trial; whether NCT05047536 is the same programme is non-obvious. Trial-identity coherence flag for human verification (no PMID supplied).

IMspire150 evaluated atezolizumab + cobimetinib + vemurafenib (BRAF-mutant melanoma) vs placebo + cobimetinib + vemurafenib. Listed under 'CHECKPOINT_MELANOMA_1L_REVIEW' is fine, but the trial is BRAF-restricted (BRAF V600+) — not a general 1L checkpoint trial. Inclusion is coherent only if the review explicitly handles BRAF-mutant subset. Flag for review-scope check, not a hard mismatch.

STICH-II's NCT is typically NCT01406548 (Mendelow 2013 Lancet). NCT00046424 corresponds to the original STICH (Mendelow 2005). Extraction's year=2017 also predates neither publication accurately. Possible NCT-trial-name binding error.
INTERACT-2 (Anderson 2013 NEJM) registered as NCT00716079. NCT01462878 is not the recognized INTERACT-2 registration. Possible NCT-name mismatch. Year=2017 in extraction is also off (true 2013).

tE/tN=220/222 (99.1% response on patiromer) and cE/cN=60/82 (73.2% on control) — AMETHYST-DN was actually a dose-finding study with three patiromer arms and *no placebo* in the published cohort; control N=82 implies a synthesized comparator. Verify whether the 82 'control' patients are from an arm not in the published Pitt 2015 cohort (NCT01514461 was originally single-arm in some versions). Possible synthesized denominator.

ZS-005 extraction has tE/tN=530/575 vs cE/cN=25/175 yielding RR ~6.45 [4.59,9.07]. ZS-005 was an open-label extension and the 'control' cohort of 175 with only 25 events looks like a non-randomized historical/observational comparator rather than a randomized control. Including it as RR with a randomized-trial-shaped row is misleading; this needs design-flag (single-arm vs RCT).

BENJAMIN pediatric marstacimab: tN=55 (treatment) vs cN=27 (control). A near-2:1 split is unusual for a hemophilia pivotal with a planned historical-OBP comparator. Verify whether the 27 'control' represent an actual randomized comparator or an observational period; likely the latter, which means the row should be flagged as single-arm + historical.

Lanadelumab HELP (Banerji 2018 JAMA) showed marked attack-rate reduction (~87% vs placebo). Extraction tE/tN=45/84 and cE/cN=35/41 yields RR=(45/84)/(35/41)=0.628; published HR=0.13 [0.07,0.24] in the row implies a rate ratio for attack count, not a binomial RR — inconsistent: the integer-count interpretation gives RR ≈ 0.63, not 0.13. tE/tN format incompatible with published HR. Likely tE/cE here are responder *counts*, not attack counts; double-encoding ambiguity.

KEYNOTE-052 is a single-arm phase 2 in cisplatin-ineligible UC (Balar 2017 Lancet Oncol). cE=cN=0 with null HR is the correct single-arm encoding, but tE=103/tN=370 still pools an event proportion that the engine may treat as comparative. Add a study-design flag = 'single_arm' so the engine excludes from pairwise pooling. (Same pattern as DEFINE-1 above; here flagging only the design-tag absence risk.)

ClarIDHy ivosidenib vs placebo (Abou-Alfa 2020 Lancet Oncol): published N=187 ITT (124 ivo / 61 placebo). Extraction's tE/tN=60/124, cE/cN=50/61 — implies 60/124=48% events on ivo vs 50/61=82% events on placebo. HR 0.37 [0.25, 0.54] is published PFS HR (correct). Event proportions consistent with PFS by IRC. OK. Flag only because tE here are PFS events, not deaths — ensure schema endpoint label is PFS not OS.

Trial name 'CAHptain-1/-2' is non-canonical; actual trial brand for crinecerfont CAH is 'CAHtalyst' (NCT04490915 for adults, NCT04806971 for pediatric). 'CAHptain' appears to be a typographic or fabricated variant. NCTs are likely correct; names need correction.

Chronocort row year=2018, NCT=NCT05000905. NCT prefix NCT05 means first posted 2021 or later; a 2018 result cannot have an NCT05 number. Year-NCT anachronism. Likely the 2018 reference is the original Mallappa/Merke paper and a different NCT, or the NCT belongs to a later confirmatory trial.
